items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has warned that credentials for Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways have been exposed, meaning attackers could potentially access organisational networks. If your workplace uses Fortinet products, IT teams should check for compromised accounts and update passwords immediately. Separately, a Cloudflare report has found that cyberattacks on civil society organisations — including Australian ones — are increasing, so community-focused and not-for-profit groups should not assume they are too small to be targeted.

A new report also highlights that Australians are widely using AI tools but that trust and safety standards are not keeping pace. Be cautious about entering sensitive work or personal information into AI platforms that haven't been approved by your organisation.

**Practical tip:** Never enter confidential client, student, or organisational data into public AI tools — check with your IT or compliance team before using any AI service for work tasks

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: Critical vulnerability in widely-used Fortinet firewalls exposes Australian infrastructure to immediate attack risk.
